constructor.name: Vector4i
constructor.description: Constructs a [Vector4i] with the default values. Equivalent to [constant ZERO].

constructor.name: Vector4i
constructor.description: Constructs a [Vector4i] from the given components: X, Y, Z, and W.

method.name: abs
method.description: Returns a new vector with all components in absolute values (i.e. positive).

method.name: clamp
method.description: Returns a new vector with all components clamped between the components of [code]min[/code] and [code]max[/code], by running [method @GlobalScope.clamp] on each component.

method.name: distance_squared_to
method.description: Returns the squared distance between this vector and [code]to[/code]. This method runs faster than [method distance_to], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

method.name: distance_to
method.description: Returns the distance between this vector and [code]to[/code].

method.name: dot
method.description: Returns the dot product of this vector and [code]with[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [code]a.dot(b)[/code] is equivalent to [code]b.dot(a)[/code].

method.name: length
method.description: Returns the length (magnitude) of this vector.

method.name: length_squared
method.description: Returns the squared length (squared magnitude) of this vector. This method runs faster than [method length], so prefer it if you need to compare vectors or need the squared distance for some formula.

method.name: max_axis_index
method.description: Returns the axis of the vector's highest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_W].

method.name: min_axis_index
method.description: Returns the axis of the vector's lowest value. See [code]AXIS_*[/code] constants. If all components are equal, this method returns [constant AXIS_X].

method.name: posmod
method.description: Returns a vector composed of the [method @GlobalScope.posmod] of this vector's components and [code]mod[/code].

method.name: posmodv
method.description: Returns a vector composed of the [method @GlobalScope.posmod] of this vector's components and [code]modv[/code]'s components.

method.name: sign
method.description: Returns a new vector with each component set to one or negative one, depending on the signs of the components, or zero if the component is zero, by calling [method @GlobalScope.sign] on each component.

members.member: The vector's W component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][3][/code].
The vector's X component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][0][/code].
The vector's Y component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][1][/code].
The vector's Z component. Also accessible by using the index position [code][2][/code].

constants.constant: Enumerated value for the X axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].
Enumerated value for the Y axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].
Enumerated value for the Z axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].
Enumerated value for the W axis. Returned by [method max_axis_index] and [method min_axis_index].
Zero vector, a vector with all components set to [code]0[/code].
One vector, a vector with all components set to [code]1[/code].
Unit vector in the X direction.
Unit vector in the Y direction.
Unit vector in the Z direction.
Unit vector in the W direction.

operator.name: operator !=
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are not equal.

operator.name: operator %
operator.description: Gets the remainder of each component of the [Vector3i] with the the given [int]. This operation uses truncated division, which is often not desired as it does not work well with negative numbers. Consider using [method @GlobalScope.posmod] instead if you want to handle negative numbers. [codeblock] print(Vector4i(10, -20, 30, -40) % 7) # Prints "(3, -6, 2, -5)" [/codeblock]

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies each component of the left [Vector4i] by each component of the right [Vector4]. Returns a [Vector4].

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies each component of the left [Vector4i] by each component of the right [Vector4i].

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector4].

operator.name: operator *
operator.description: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4i] by the given [int].

operator.name: operator +
operator.description: Adds each component of the left [Vector4i] with each component of the right [Vector4]. Returns a [Vector4].

operator.name: operator +
operator.description: Adds each component of the left [Vector4i] with each component of the right [Vector4i].

operator.name: operator -
operator.description: Subtracts each component of the left [Vector4i] by each component of the right [Vector4]. Returns a [Vector4].

operator.name: operator -
operator.description: Subtracts each component of the left [Vector4i] by each component of the right [Vector4i].

operator.name: operator /
operator.description: Divides each component of the [Vector4i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector4].

operator.name: operator /
operator.description: Divides each component of the [Vector4i] by the given [int].

operator.name: operator ==
operator.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the vectors are equal.

operator.name: operator []
operator.description: Access vector components by index. [code]v[0][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.x[/code], [code]v[1][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.y[/code], [code]v[2][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.z[/code], and [code]v[3][/code] is equivalent to [code]v.w[/code].

operator.name: operator unary-
operator.description: Returns the negative value of the [Vector4i]. This is the same as writing [code]Vector4i(-v.x, -v.y, -v.z, -v.w)[/code]. This operation flips the direction of the vector while keeping the same magnitude.
